danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazards throughout levels (referenced in gameplay)

danger_signals.meaning: Magical antagonist actively pursuing; fate-altering threat

Resource availability; player can recover/gain advantages
Allies/aid present; progression enabled through mystical means

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Manifests as controllable shadow entity after red temple sequence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Associated with darkness, night, magical flame

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Royal lineage; father's sword; magic flame
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually transformed into beggar appearance by magic; contrasts with Jaffar's false noble form
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Moves through diverse environments; carries symbolic items (sword, flame)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Jaffar has seized the throne in Prince's form

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Palace guards automatically hostile to unrecognized Prince; Jaffar commands authority; Princess under death spell (visual illness/incapacitation)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character appearance changed to beggar; nobody recognizes him
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs treat Prince as threat/vagrant; forced to flee by guards; cannot communicate royal identity

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Prince is exiled on foreign island
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition from palace to cave environment; shift to darker, supernatural aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Encounters undead enemies; must fight through cursed spaces; journey becomes epic/quest-like

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Prince discovers his true heritage and power
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red temple environment; bird-monks kneel; shadow becomes controllable; magic flame obtained
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies submit; player gains new abilities; transformation from fugitive to hero in visual language

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jaffar burned by magic flame; Princess awakens; return to palace/reunion imagery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies cease; peaceful resolution; cycle of restoration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Old witch watching through crystal ball (final image)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ominous surveillance imagery; foreshadows future conflict; cliffhanger mood

environmental_storytelling.example: Progression from formal palace → island cave → ancient ruins (Basra) → red temple → back to palace. Each location visually communicates world history and magical corruption layers.
Reanimated skeletons indicate cursed/necromantic space; flying goblins signal chaotic magic; bird-monks indicate organized mystical order. Enemy visual design reveals space purpose.
Basra shown as 'ruins of' previous civilization; throne room architecture deteriorated; indicates magical collapse of former order.
Father's sword recovered in ruins; magic flame obtained in temple; these items visually represent player power growth and heritage reclamation.
Prince's beggar disguise vs. Jaffar's stolen noble form; visual contrast communicates identity theft and social reversal theme.
Magic carpet, magical horse, flying enemies, shadow entity all communicate world operates under magical laws; not natural/grounded reality.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Automatically hostile; pursue on sight; no negotiation possible
Attack on encounter; no dialogue; mindless aggression
Active hostility; aerial pursuit; screaming vocalizations indicate chaos/panic

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Red temple - before Prince obtains flame
Red temple - after Prince wields shadow and flame
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Kneel/submit; no longer hostile; acknowledge Prince's power
Commands authority; orders Prince's capture; uses magical storms as weapons; actively opposes protagonist
Incapacitated/ill; non-interactive; visual indicator of villain's power; motivates protagonist
Surveillance through crystal ball; no direct action; ominous presence indicating hidden threats

notes: Prince of Persia 2 communicates world-state primarily through environmental design, enemy type diversity, and character transformation. The game uses visual hierarchies (palace → wilderness → ruins → temple) to map narrative progression. Magical systems are visualized through supernatural enemy types and mystical items rather than explicit UI elements. Faction identity relies on location context and enemy appearance rather than color-coding or banners. The 'disguise' mechanic—where the Prince appears as beggar while Jaffar wears his form—is central to political world-state communication, establishing themes of identity theft and illegitimate rule. The presence of the Shadow as both antagonist and eventually player-controlled tool reflects psychological/magical duality in the world. Time limit (75 minutes real-time) creates implicit danger signal throughout all levels.
